summary: Dropbox cut about 500 employees and explicitly framed the move around slowing growth and the arrival of the AI era.
evidenceQuote: I've made the difficult decision to reduce our global workforce by about 16%, or 500 Dropboxers... Second, and more consequentially, the AI era of computing has finally arrived.
notes: Primary CEO memo directly links the layoff to the AI transition; also captured by TechCrunch and CNBC.

sources.name: Dropbox Blog
sources.quote: I've made the difficult decision to reduce our global workforce by about 16%, or 500 Dropboxers... Second, and more consequentially, the AI era of computing has finally arrived.

sources.name: TechCrunch
sources.quote: Dropbox today announced that it would be laying off 16% of its staff, equivalent to about 500 employees, due to slowing growth, and in the words of CEO Drew Houston, because the AI era of computing has finally arrived.

summary: Reuters reported BT Group planned to cut up to 55,000 jobs by 2030, with automation and AI expected to replace another 10,000 roles.
evidenceQuote: BT Group, Britain's biggest broadband and mobile provider, will cut up to 55,000 jobs including contractors by 2030 as it completes its fibre roll-out and adapts to new technologies such as AI.
notes: Reuters reported BT explicitly said technologies like automation and AI would replace another 10,000 jobs within the broader workforce reduction plan.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.quote: Jansen said around 10,000 fewer network engineers would be needed to run digital networks, while technologies like automation and AI would replace another 10,000.

summary: SAP announced a restructuring affecting about 8,000 positions while shifting more resources into Business AI and AI-driven efficiencies.
evidenceQuote: In 2024, SAP will further increase its focus on key strategic growth areas, in particular Business AI... SAP plans to execute a company-wide restructuring program in 2024. The majority of the approximately 8,000 affected positions is expected to be covered by voluntary leave programs and internal re-skilling measures.
notes: Strong primary-source case: SAP explicitly paired the restructuring with Business AI focus and AI-driven efficiencies.

sources.name: SAP News Center
sources.quote: In 2024, SAP will further increase its focus on key strategic growth areas, in particular Business AI... SAP plans to execute a company-wide restructuring program in 2024. The majority of the approximately 8,000 affected positions is expected to be covered by voluntary leave programs and internal re-skilling measures.

summary: Intuit said about 1,800 employees would leave as it accelerated investment in AI-native products, data and AI, and its AI-driven expert platform.
evidenceQuote: Companies that aren't prepared to take advantage of this AI revolution will fall behind... Today we will be communicating to approximately 1,800 employees, which is 10% of our workforce, that they will be leaving Intuit... We will accelerate investing in data and AI, leveraging GenOS to reimagine our products from traditional workflows to AI-native experiences.
notes: Primary filing / employee memo makes the AI-investment rationale explicit and quantifies the workforce reduction.

sources.name: Intuit SEC filing / employee memo
sources.quote: Companies that aren't prepared to take advantage of this AI revolution will fall behind... Today we will be communicating to approximately 1,800 employees, which is 10% of our workforce, that they will be leaving Intuit... We will accelerate investing in data and AI, leveraging GenOS to reimagine our products from traditional workflows to AI-native experiences.

sources.name: Associated Press
sources.quote: Tax preparation and financial software company Intuit announced an AI-focused reorganization plan Wednesday that includes laying off about 10% of its workforce.

summary: Workday said it would reduce its workforce by about 1,750 employees while continuing to invest aggressively in AI and platform innovation.
evidenceQuote: Today, we are announcing a workforce reduction of approximately 1,750 employees, or 8.5% of our current workforce.
notes: Primary company statement also said Workday would continue investing aggressively in AI and platform innovation.

sources.name: Workday
sources.quote: Today, we are announcing a workforce reduction of approximately 1,750 employees, or 8.5% of our current workforce.

summary: DBS Group said it planned to cut about 4,000 roles over three years as AI takes on work currently done by humans.
evidenceQuote: DBS Group plans to cut 4,000 jobs over the next three years as it expects artificial intelligence (AI) increasingly take on roles carried out by humans.
notes: Reuters-backed report also said DBS expected to add about 1,000 AI-related jobs.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.quote: DBS Group plans to cut 4,000 jobs over the next three years as it expects artificial intelligence (AI) increasingly take on roles carried out by humans.

summary: Chegg said it would lay off about 22% of its workforce as students increasingly turned to AI-powered tools such as ChatGPT over traditional edtech platforms.
evidenceQuote: Chegg said on Monday it would lay off about 22% of its workforce, or 248 employees, to cut costs and streamline its operations as students increasingly turn to AI-powered tools such as ChatGPT over traditional edtech platforms.
notes: Reuters tied the layoffs directly to AI competition, including ChatGPT and Google's AI Overviews.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.quote: Chegg said on Monday it would lay off about 22% of its workforce, or 248 employees, to cut costs and streamline its operations as students increasingly turn to AI-powered tools such as ChatGPT over traditional edtech platforms.

summary: Reuters reported TomTom would cut 300 jobs as it realigned the company and embraced artificial intelligence as part of its product-led strategy.
evidenceQuote: Dutch location technology company TomTom said on Monday it would cut 300 jobs as it realigns its organization and embraces artificial intelligence as part of its product-led strategy.
notes: Strong Reuters item with direct company language tying the cuts to an AI-led organizational shift.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.quote: Dutch location technology company TomTom said on Monday it would cut 300 jobs as it realigns its organization and embraces artificial intelligence as part of its product-led strategy.

summary: Recruit Holdings said it would cut about 1,300 jobs at Indeed and Glassdoor as it adapted its hiring products around AI.
evidenceQuote: Recruit Holdings, the Japanese parent of Indeed and Glassdoor, said on Friday that it is laying off about 1,300 employees, or 6% of the staff, at the two job sites.
notes: TechCrunch, citing Reuters reporting and an internal memo, said the company was adapting around AI.

sources.name: TechCrunch
sources.quote: Recruit Holdings, the Japanese parent of Indeed and Glassdoor, said on Friday that it is laying off about 1,300 employees, or 6% of the staff, at the two job sites.

summary: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff said the company cut 4,000 customer support roles as AI reduced the need for headcount.
evidenceQuote: Salesforce has cut 4,000 of its customer support roles, CEO Marc Benioff recently said while discussing how artificial intelligence has helped reduce the company headcount.

sources.quote: Salesforce has cut 4,000 of its customer support roles, CEO Marc Benioff recently said while discussing how artificial intelligence has helped reduce the company headcount.

summary: Just Eat said it would lay off around 450 employees while integrating automation and AI into its operations.

sources.quote: Just Eat is integrating automation and artificial intelligence into its operations, including transitioning manual customer service tasks to automated systems, it said.

evidenceQuote: Meta is also cutting around 600 positions out of the several thousand roles in its Superintelligence Labs, the social media platforms owner said in October.

sources.quote: Meta is also cutting around 600 positions out of the several thousand roles in its Superintelligence Labs, the social media platforms owner said in October.

sources.quote: The U.S. computer and printer manufacturer said last November it expects to cut 4,000 to 6,000 jobs globally by fiscal 2028 as it streamlines operations and adopts AI.

evidenceQuote: The German insurance group plans to cut up to 1,800 jobs in its travel insurance division as AI increasingly replaces manual processes, a source familiar with the plans told Reuters last November.
notes: Included in Reuters' February 2026 roundup of AI-linked layoffs.

evidenceQuote: The sportswear giant is laying off 775 employees, a source familiar with the matter told Reuters in January, as the firm looks to boost profits and accelerate its use of automation.

sources.quote: The sportswear giant is laying off 775 employees, a source familiar with the matter told Reuters in January, as the firm looks to boost profits and accelerate its use of automation.

sources.quote: The owner of Facebook and Instagram is cutting over 1,000 jobs at its Reality Labs unit in a pivot from the Metaverse to AI devices, Bloomberg reported on Jan. 13.

evidenceQuote: The U.S. design-software maker said on Jan. 22 it would shed about 7% of its global workforce, or roughly 1,000 jobs, as it redirects spending to its cloud platform and AI initiatives.

sources.quote: The U.S. design-software maker said on Jan. 22 it would shed about 7% of its global workforce, or roughly 1,000 jobs, as it redirects spending to its cloud platform and AI initiatives.

evidenceQuote: Pinterest said Tuesday it plans to lay off less than 15% of its workforce and cut back on office space as the company embraces artificial intelligence.
notes: CNBC reported Pinterest had more than 4,500 employees globally as of its most recent proxy filing.

sources.name: CNBC
sources.quote: Pinterest said Tuesday it plans to lay off less than 15% of its workforce and cut back on office space as the company embraces artificial intelligence.

summary: Amazon confirmed 16,000 corporate job cuts as part of an efficiency drive that Reuters tied directly to the company's rising AI adoption.
evidenceQuote: The latest cuts mark the second major round of layoffs in three months after Amazon pared 14,000 jobs in October, saying at the time that artificial intelligence and concerns over shifting corporate culture were to blame.
notes: Reuters also reported Andy Jassy had said rising use of AI tools would mean more automation of duties and corporate job losses.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.quote: The latest cuts mark the second major round of layoffs in three months after Amazon pared 14,000 jobs in October, saying at the time that artificial intelligence and concerns over shifting corporate culture were to blame.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.quote: The U.S. chemical producer said on January 29 it will slash about 4,500 jobs, 13% of its total workforce, as it streamlines all its end-to-end work processes by using automation and AI.

sources.quote: India's Ola Electric said on Friday it would lay off 5% of its workforce in a restructuring effort aimed at improving profitability through greater automation.

evidenceQuote: The Australian software company said on Feb. 25 it will axe about 2,000 jobs, nearly one-third of its global workforce, as it integrates AI into customer software and internal operations.

sources.quote: The Australian software company said on Feb. 25 it will axe about 2,000 jobs, nearly one-third of its global workforce, as it integrates AI into customer software and internal operations.

evidenceQuote: The French small appliance and cookware maker said on February 25 it would launch a restructuring plan that also takes "full advantage" of the possibilities offered by AI that may impact up to 2,100 jobs worldwide by 2027.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.quote: The French small appliance and cookware maker said on February 25 it would launch a restructuring plan that also takes "full advantage" of the possibilities offered by AI that may impact up to 2,100 jobs worldwide by 2027.

evidenceQuote: Block said it will cut over 4,000 jobs, nearly half its workforce, as part of an overhaul to embed artificial intelligence across its operations.
notes: Reuters reported the move as a major AI-linked workforce cut.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.quote: Block said it will cut over 4,000 jobs, nearly half its workforce, as part of an overhaul to embed artificial intelligence across its operations.

summary: CrowdStrike said it would cut about 500 jobs as AI-driven efficiencies flattened its hiring curve and reshaped how it operates.
evidenceQuote: AI flattens our hiring curve, and helps us innovate from idea to product faster. It streamlines go-to-market, improves customer outcomes, and drives efficiencies across both the front and back office.
notes: CEO George Kurtz tied the 5% workforce cut directly to AI in a memo attached to the SEC filing; Reuters roundup and trade coverage echoed the linkage.

sources.name: CrowdStrike SEC filing / CEO memo
sources.quote: AI flattens our hiring curve, and helps us innovate from idea to product faster. It streamlines go-to-market, improves customer outcomes, and drives efficiencies across both the front and back office.

sources.name: Cybersecurity Dive
sources.quote: CrowdStrike said Wednesday that it would cut 500 jobs, or 5% of its global workforce, as part of a plan to scale its business and meet a goal of  billion in annual recurring revenue, according to a regulatory filing.

summary: Chegg said it would cut 388 jobs, about 45% of its workforce, as the company said the new realities of AI had sharply reduced traffic and revenue.
evidenceQuote: Educational technology company Chegg said on Monday it would cut 388 roles globally, or about 45% of the workforce, to reduce costs and streamline operations as it works to adapt to the growing shift toward AI-powered tools.
notes: Distinct from Chegg's May 2025 layoff round; Reuters said Chegg blamed the new realities of AI and reduced Google traffic to publishers.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.quote: Educational technology company Chegg said on Monday it would cut 388 roles globally, or about 45% of the workforce, to reduce costs and streamline operations as it works to adapt to the growing shift toward AI-powered tools.

summary: Grammarly laid off about 230 employees and said the restructuring would deepen its technical investments in AI and push toward an AI-enabled workplace.
evidenceQuote: As we strengthen our focus toward driving the AI-enabled workplace and deepen our technical investments in AI, we will need a different mix of capabilities and skillsets.
notes: Company framed the cuts as a restructuring for the AI-enabled workplace rather than a cost-cutting exercise.

sources.name: TechCrunch
sources.quote: Grammarly is laying off 230 employees worldwide as part of a "business restructuring"... The layoffs are part of Grammarly's efforts to advance its focus on "the AI-enabled workplace of the future."

sources.name: Business Insider
sources.quote: Roy-Chowdhury insisted that the company's "financial position is, and remains, strong." Instead, he said the layoffs had been conducted as a means of recalibrating resources for ?the AI-enabled workplace of the future.?

summary: Cisco said it would cut about 7% of its workforce as it restructured to invest more heavily in AI, cybersecurity, and related growth areas.
evidenceQuote: California technology company Cisco says it is cutting 7% of its workforce as it shifts its focus to AI, cybersecurity.
notes: AP and Network World both tied the August 2024 restructuring directly to Cisco's AI push and resource reallocation into AI growth areas.

sources.name: Associated Press via ABC News
sources.quote: California technology company Cisco says it is cutting 7% of its workforce as it shifts its focus to AI, cybersecurity.

sources.name: Network World
sources.quote: Cisco is trying to move more quickly into three core areas ? AI networking, security and collaboration... "We actually are shifting hundreds of millions of dollars into AI," Robbins said.

summary: Recruit Holdings cut about 1,300 jobs across Indeed and Glassdoor while telling staff it had to adapt to AI-driven change in hiring and product development.
